text.title: AI Accessibility Automation 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-accessibility-automation-2026 methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: axe-playwright (or equivalent) MUST run on every deploy and emit a structured violation report. Skipping scans on `quick` deploys is forbidden.
rule.rationale: A11y regressions land at deploy time; without per-deploy scans the regression window stretches into weeks.

rule.statement: AI ranks and de-duplicates violations, but it MUST NOT decide what enters the backlog. A human a11y lead reviews the ranked list and approves the cut.
rule.rationale: AI ranking is statistically good but contextually blind; only the human lead knows which low-severity items are deal-breakers for a specific customer.

rule.statement: AI-generated VPAT 2.5 drafts MUST be signed by the human a11y lead before being shared with customers or regulators. Unsigned drafts MUST carry a `DRAFT — NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION` watermark.
rule.rationale: VPAT carries legal liability; AI hallucination in a VPAT can trigger ADA litigation. Human sign-off is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: AI-generated alt text MUST be accepted only when the model's confidence ≥ 0.8 AND the image is decorative-or-content-low-stakes. Editorial images go to a human writer.
rule.rationale: Low-stakes alt text (decorative product shots) tolerates AI; editorial / news / branded images do not.

rule.statement: Auto-generated captions MUST be human-reviewed at a stratified rate: 100% for legal/medical/financial content, 20% sample for other content. Captions ship only after the relevant review.
rule.rationale: Unreviewed captions on regulated content carry the same liability as unreviewed transcripts; stratified sampling is the only defensible balance.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
